description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a dark gray, weathered surface. One side of the block features intricate carvings, possibly depicting stylized leaves or wings, suggesting historical or decorative significance. The other sides are mostly unadorned, showing natural stone texture with some chipping and irregularities. The block appears solid and heavy, with a coarse, matte finish typical of quarried stone. Its top surface is relatively flat, making it suitable for supporting other objects or being used as a seat or step. The overall appearance suggests it may have once been part of a larger architectural or ornamental structure.
description: A dark gray, weathered stone block with one side featuring ornate carvings and the rest showing rough, natural stone texture. The top is flat and the block appears solid and heavy.
description_view_0: This view shows a plain, rough stone surface with no visible carvings or decorations.
description_view_1: This view reveals ornate carvings resembling leaves or wings on one face of the block, adding decorative detail.
description_view_2: The carved side is most prominent here, with deep relief patterns and more visible chipping along the edges.
description_view_3: This view returns to a mostly plain, rough stone surface with some minor chipping and irregularities.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or plaza with decorative stonework
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on compacted earth
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible chipping on edges and a generally rough, aged surface; no significant moss or water smoothing.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Ornate carvings resembling leaves or wings are present on one side.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: appears isolated but could be aligned with other decorative blocks
